[2020-11-20 14:56] elyot: and sometimes after 4 or 5 turns the opening is barely played but you feel like you've already lost and future turns are just going through the motions
[2020-11-20 14:57] elyot: well you can still play through games in your head or just open another analysis board or whatever
[2020-11-20 14:57] elyot: if you have infinite time and you're not locked into one game at a time then it's much easier to analyze
[2020-11-20 14:58] masn6811 (Masne98888): id be incredibly impressed if someone could analyze more than 2 turns in the future in their head, even with 10 minutes
[2020-11-20 14:58] silentslayers: Yeah MasN, your computer is locked and you can't access prismata from any device once you start a game, so analyzing is impossible.. Kappa
[2020-11-20 14:58] silentslayers: obviously you just open a different client or something
[2020-11-20 14:58] elyot: hahah honestly we got very good at analyzing several moves ahead back in the early days
[2020-11-20 14:59] elyot: Prismata is more complex now so it's less viable in most games that are not like, grindy red rushes
[2020-11-20 14:59] elyot: I still think 3-4 turns ahead in openings but you can get away with it when there's less interaction on the board
[2020-11-20 15:00] masn6811 (Masne98888): i mean, you can analyze in ranked right now with a second client and a smurf, its just incredibly inconvinent to do so, therefore people dont do it
so if you made it even more inconvinient, people wouldnt even analyze in async
[2020-11-20 15:01] silentslayers: Yeah but I assume it wouldn't even lock your original client if this was implemented
[2020-11-20 15:01] silentslayers: It would be weird if it was made inconvenient imo
[2020-11-20 15:02] masn6811 (Masne98888): i mean, honestly, 10 turns in, to analyze youd have to replay the 10 turns of moves required to get there. and you'd have to remember the 10 turns of moves as well, because its not a replay yet
[2020-11-20 15:02] masn6811 (Masne98888): that seems incredibly inconvient to me
[2020-11-20 15:03] silentslayers: I guess, because you chose a number 10, this arguement sounds ok, but you can also do that at any point up until 10, like 5 or 7
[2020-11-20 15:03] silentslayers: you could also just analyze the unit set
[2020-11-20 15:04] masn6811 (Masne98888): @Elyot when balancing units, do you care more about player balance, autowin frequency, or must-buys
[2020-11-20 15:06] masn6811 (Masne98888): eg. doomed drone and centurion lead to fairly balanced games, but are often must-buys
trinity drone is a quite weak unit, but is notiously known for being p2-favored
iceblade doesn't favor either player paticularly, nor is it always a must-buy, but when it is, leads to a number of non-games
[2020-11-20 15:07] elyot: It's no one single thing, we want the puzzle of "what should I build" to be interesting as often as possible and we want unique and fun situations to emerge naturally
[2020-11-20 15:08] elyot: like all 3 of those problems can be issues but it depends on the severity
[2020-11-20 15:08] masn6811 (Masne98888): isn't thorium very problematic then? "what should I build" is rarely a puzzle when that unit is in the set
[2020-11-20 15:09] elyot: no, thorium creates more variety in openings because the combinatorics are different and the green options change things up
[2020-11-20 15:10] elyot: like Prismata with only basic drones has fewer types of games than Prismata with a variety of different drones that change up the openings or provide new options
[2020-11-20 15:10] masn6811 (Masne98888): thorium openings are almost always DD/DD D1/11. in addition, the fact that thorium provides such easy and efficient tech-fixing both in and out of green makes "should i go green" a non-decision
[2020-11-20 15:12] elyot: ok but those openings make t3 and t4 different than typical openings, so there are new situations to explore and optimize around. Like "the evil animus" exists only because of Thorium, and understanding it is a "eureka moment" for players
[2020-11-20 15:13] elyot: like the real goal of the Prismata unit set is to have as many "eureka moments" for players of all skill levels for as long as possible
[2020-11-20 15:13] elyot: if all games use the typical drones then there is a less total sum of eureka moments because the openings are less diverse
[2020-11-20 15:14] p0lari (Polari): you're using the same words to refer to diversity between all different prismata games (elyot) and diversity of options in pools involving the unit (masn)
[2020-11-20 15:14] masn6811 (Masne98888): whats the eureka moment behind the evil animus? maybe im wooshed, i tried it a few times, and it feels rarely good
[2020-11-20 15:15] elyot: it doesn't have to be "oh this is a good strat"
[2020-11-20 15:15] p0lari (Polari): if you make a stupidly overpowered econ unit that completely warps the builds and is bought 100% then yeah it adds diversity in the sense that the games with that unit in them play differently than ones without it
[2020-11-20 15:15] elyot: it can just be "oh this is an idea, which has this outcome, which might be good sometimes, but even if it isn't, it still has an interesting property"
[2020-11-20 15:15] p0lari (Polari): the disagreement is about whether that kind of "diversity" is a good thing
[2020-11-20 15:16] masn6811 (Masne98888): okay, what was the interesitng property of the evil animus?
[2020-11-20 15:16] p0lari (Polari): I intended that description to be a hyperbole but looking back that's actually a pretty accurate description of thorium
[2020-11-20 15:16] masn6811 (Masne98888): also, my opinion wrt. thorium is that if it was balanced at a slightly weaker level so it was only bought like 60% or so of times, you'd still be able to have the weird builds when it is bought, but not be forced to just buy it every time it appears
[2020-11-20 15:17] elyot: "eureka moments" are the currency that "interestingness" is expressed in, they are the elements of strategy and puzzle games that make players continue to play, they up-regulate memory formation and incentivize the behaviour of strategic exploration
[2020-11-20 15:18] elyot: like at a very basic level it can just be "oh if I go animus here, my opponent can't absorb"
[2020-11-20 15:18] elyot: at a higher level, it's a whole new situation to think about in terms of cost/benefit and build order planning
[2020-11-20 15:20] elyot: a "eureka moment" can be anything from like, the most basic thing (walls can absorb and absorb is good) to more subtle things (a combination of walls and engineers is better than all walls or all engineers) to really esoteric things (prompt is sometimes not a desirable property and there are exactly the following situations/reasons for it...)
[2020-11-20 15:21] masn6811 (Masne98888): another balance question. for centurion, why is it specifically 18, and not 17 or 19?
[2020-11-20 15:21] Deleted User (Deleted User): 18 divides a lot of numbers
[2020-11-20 15:22] elyot: it's not that, it's mostly that we nerfed it a bunch and then decided not to nerf it anymore once it hit 18
[2020-11-20 15:22] awaclus: my favorite eureka moment in Prismata is when you realize that Cryo Ray is sometimes better than Gauss Charge because you can choose the Cryo Ray's target
[2020-11-20 15:22] nvlx (Fate): truths are boring :FeelsBadMan:
[2020-11-20 15:22] masn6811 (Masne98888): but why not nerf it anymore? i assume the reason it was nerfed was because it was just an auto-buy, and it's still an auto-buy
[2020-11-20 15:23] elyot: we actually used to call them "MCDS moments" before Prismata had its current name
[2020-11-20 15:23] elyot: so the goal with centurion is not to like, make its buyrate 50% or 30% or some target
[2020-11-20 15:23] elyot: it's more nuanced than that
[2020-11-20 15:25] elyot: like say it was 20 instead of 18, honestly the buyrate doesn't change much, but it gets a bit more awkward and creates more feelbad turns, is it worth making the change?

[2020-11-20 15:25] Deleted User (Deleted User): how good would it have to be to reach 99-100% butrate
[2020-11-20 15:25] Deleted User (Deleted User): i think it changes a lot
[2020-11-20 15:25] Deleted User (Deleted User): 2 gold is massive surely
[2020-11-20 15:26] masn6811 (Masne98888): i think it's more than worth it. it changes the buyrate like a fair amount. and by fair amount, like the difference between 95% and 99% is a factor of 5
[2020-11-20 15:27] masn6811 (Masne98888): like the advantage of having a unit in the set is it gives you more options. you can either buy it, or not buy it. but if a unit is so good you're forced to buy it every time, then that's not really an option
[2020-11-20 15:27] awaclus: if you can get it to 90-93%, then you can say that it's more skippable than people think
[2020-11-20 15:27] Deleted User (Deleted User): interesting but not necessarily that surprising
[2020-11-20 15:28] elyot: it's a bit higher now
[2020-11-20 15:29] masn6811 (Masne98888): im surprised actually. i can think of some sets where something super degenerate happens and you dont buy it
but its so powerful that its often correct to buy it even in the precense of say brooder or vai
[2020-11-20 15:29] Deleted User (Deleted User): its more like arka or rush combos
[2020-11-20 15:29] elyot: im trying to access more recent stats file and I'm getting a 404 on dropbox
[2020-11-20 15:31] masn6811 (Masne98888): i mean i think what's interesting is not just that, but *when* centurion is skipped.
like if you look at sets where centurion is skipped and you see its all wild drone iceblade or blood pact tatsu or something
[2020-11-20 15:32] elyot: ok but making centurion more expensive doesn't fix that
[2020-11-20 15:32] elyot: with RGB units or colourless units we're less worried about higher buyrates
[2020-11-20 15:32] masn6811 (Masne98888): well, it might also make it not worth it in cases of brooder, vai, etc.
[2020-11-20 15:33] masn6811 (Masne98888): and honestly, i think you should be *very* worried about high buyrates for RGB units
[2020-11-20 15:33] masn6811 (Masne98888): because that implies that each player has to buy CBA - the techbase is fixed
[2020-11-20 15:33] masn6811 (Masne98888): and the tech choice imo is one of the most interesting aspects of the game
[2020-11-20 15:34] masn6811 (Masne98888): on the other hand, the current iteration of centrifuge, which has been heavily nerfed, creates a ton of feelsbad moments for new players trying to use it
[2020-11-20 15:35] elyot: yeah I'm not sure if we ultimately went in the correct direction with it

[2020-11-20 15:40] elyot: could but we usually just test them on our test server
[2020-11-20 15:41] elyot: if there was some kind of community-created balance patch then maybe we could use a weekly event to test it
[2020-11-20 15:42] masn6811 (Masne98888): i believe the test server is just for devs and luminaries? how active is it?
[2020-11-20 15:42] elyot: dead unless we're testing something basically

[2020-11-20 19:01] megasupp (mega-supp): @Elyot you said you were trying to figure out how to *make Prismata really big* . Have you considered doing something like what Zenonzard did? basically in zenonzard there is an AI helper that suggests you moves. and when you deviate it "learns and adapts to your playstyle". It might help with the learning curve which I think is the main factor that drives away new players.
[2020-11-20 19:08] Deleted User (Deleted User): i think i said smth similar abt this
[2020-11-20 19:08] megasupp (mega-supp): I think instead of suggesting specific moves it should just try to prevent players from commiting common mistakes. Like suggesting to buy tech for an absorber when opponent will likely have attackers in 2 turns. or telling to buy engis to stay granular. or warning them when they are gonna overtech themselves
[2020-11-20 19:09] Deleted User (Deleted User): > !! apparently in a game called Zenonzard there's this thing called asynch pvp where the AI builds "profiles" of players and uses machine learning to build decks and come up with moves etc and on ladder you don't play against someone but instead on an AI who plays like the person
> if u could do this in pris i think it would change everything
> and it helps new players as well since the AI gives tips on what moves to make
https://discord.com/channels/112616041175089152/391209800911814656/721084323163406376
[2020-11-20 19:09] megasupp (mega-supp): the discussion was about asyncronous pvp
[2020-11-20 19:09] Deleted User (Deleted User): and here was eloyot's response:

> I kinda think you'd have to solve a problem that's almost as hard as just making a machine learning AI for Prismata
> it might help to emulate humans for deckbuilding, but for actual gameplay, it's really hard
> Prismata is a game that humans are so good at, they can really identify when the AI is not playing well (unlike in hidden information card games where AI misplays are way more concealed)
[2020-11-20 19:12] megasupp (mega-supp): I think that just identifying some cases where newbies make mistakes  with some decent accuracy is not that hard for AI

[2020-11-26 17:46] Deleted User (Deleted User): Are dicts considered better than classes for cards/units?
[2020-11-26 17:47] Deleted User (Deleted User): I have been using classes exclusively so far. Curious whether they're worse/better
[2020-11-26 17:48] elyot: I don't know what you mean, you mean literally just as a data structure for storing them? It almost certainly doesn't matter at all if you're working in any reasonable language.
[2020-11-26 17:49] elyot: In fact that json code gets "compiled" to a dictionary on the server-side and a class on the client-side 😛
[2020-11-26 17:49] Deleted User (Deleted User): Ah ok, that's pretty cool
[2020-11-26 17:50] elyot: If the only thing that you need is to index by name, there are tons of data structures that will let you do that and the running time is almost never going to matter so just use whatever is convenient
[2020-11-26 17:51] elyot: if you are gonna do some operation that requires millions of lookups and needs to be fast then there's a more interesting conversation to be had, but probably that isn't the case if you're just making a card game

[2020-11-28 22:44] elyot: BTW my comments on the changes:
- Centrifuge: Ugh... I agree that the current one sucks, but I still have a very strong memory of why the previous one was changed. I don't think going back to it is the right solution, but maybe there's just nothing better.
- Centurion: It feels like a needless change to me, because it's not oppressive like Odin or the other high-cost units; it just becomes a unit that both people buy and adding to its cost does little. In cases where it's not buyable at 20 but is at 18, it just gets priced out of the game. I don't think there are really situations where it's buyable at 20 but somehow the extra cost adds interesting decisions (e.g. about when to buy it or something).
- Smorcus: Haven't decided yet. Pay-gold-for-attack is a problem for the AI in that it can create a circular resource dependency. It can also create states that can only be reached via undo (e.g. say you have drone, animus, smorcus, and auride. You click Drone, Smorcus, Auride, undo Smorcus, and you've reached an outcome that the AI will never play and a human can never play without hitting undo). In the past we've avoided printing that. Nerfing 1 gold seems to take the edge off the unit, but it seems warranted?
- Grimbotch: This really feels like it takes the power from the unit, like it's no longer a "strong unit that's weak to chill", it's just an average unit. I generally don't like those kind of changes; I want more units to feel strong, not less. Maybe I don't understand what's oppressive about the old unit, but if the answer is "both players end up buying all of them in big sets", then it doesn't matter. If the answer is "it leads to red rush mirrors", then IMO this isn't a bad thing, I think we have too few of them now.
- Electrovore: This type of change is usually easy to implement because missions/puzzles usually don't depend on the HP of the unit, but vore might be one of the few cases for which it matters in several instances. I don't get why it matters tho.

messages.content:
BTW my comments on the changes:
- Centrifuge: Ugh... I agree that the current one sucks, but I still have a very strong memory of why the previous one was changed. I don't think going back to it is the right solution, but maybe there's just nothing better.
- Centurion: It feels like a needless change to me, because it's not oppressive like Odin or the other high-cost units; it just becomes a unit that both people buy and adding to its cost does little. In cases where it's not buyable at 20 but is at 18, it just gets priced out of the game. I don't think there are really situations where it's buyable at 20 but somehow the extra cost adds interesting decisions (e.g. about when to buy it or something).
- Smorcus: Haven't decided yet. Pay-gold-for-attack is a problem for the AI in that it can create a circular resource dependency. It can also create states that can only be reached via undo (e.g. say you have drone, animus, smorcus, and auride. You click Drone, Smorcus, Auride, undo Smorcus, and you've reached an outcome that the AI will never play and a human can never play without hitting undo). In the past we've avoided printing that. Nerfing 1 gold seems to take the edge off the unit, but it seems warranted?
- Grimbotch: This really feels like it takes the power from the unit, like it's no longer a "strong unit that's weak to chill", it's just an average unit. I generally don't like those kind of changes; I want more units to feel strong, not less. Maybe I don't understand what's oppressive about the old unit, but if the answer is "both players end up buying all of them in big sets", then it doesn't matter. If the answer is "it leads to red rush mirrors", then IMO this isn't a bad thing, I think we have too few of them now.
- Electrovore: This type of change is usually easy to implement because missions/puzzles usually don't depend on the HP of the unit, but vore might be one of the few cases for which it matters in several instances. I don't get why it matters tho.
